fault_hypothesis: Line 67 uses non-strict comparisons (`b <= s` and `a >= e`) for the overlap check instead of strict comparisons (`b < s` and `a > e`), causing touching/adjacent windows to be incorrectly accepted instead of rejected.

spec.expr: all(not (b__1 < s or a__1 > e) == (not (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e)) for s, e in timeline if b__1 != s and a__1 != e)

constraints.intent: The strict and non-strict overlap checks should agree when no endpoint touching occurs; when they disagree (touching case), the non-strict version is wrong.

spec.expr: all((b__1 < s or a__1 > e) == (b__1 <= s or a__1 >= e) or (b__1 == s or a__1 == e) for s, e in timeline)

constraints.intent: The strict and non-strict no-overlap conditions differ only when an endpoint of the new window exactly touches an endpoint of an existing slot.

spec.expr: all(not (result__1[i][1] == result__1[i+1][0]) for i in range(len(result__1)-1))

constraints.intent: In the correctly inserted result, no two adjacent slots should share an endpoint (touching slots should have been rejected before reaching this point).

spec.expr: not (__cbfl_result[0] == True and any(window[1] == s or window[0] == e for s, e in timeline))

constraints.intent: If the new window touches any existing slot endpoint, the function should not return success=True.
